request.question: Break down Jahez's FY2025 unit economics from #FY2025-Earnings-Release — GMV growth vs take rate, AOV, contribution margin, EBITDA margin — and why did net income compress ~61% despite double-digit GMV growth?

events.text:
## Jahez FY2025 Unit Economics — Decomposition

**B
ottom line:** GMV grew a healthy 10.8%, and take rate actually expanded, but the
P&L below gross profit was eroded by a deliberate marketing push, a
step-up in D&A, and a spike in credit losses/impairments — driving the

~61% net income compression.

---

### 1. Volume & GMV bridge
In FY2025, GMV increased 10.8% YoY to SAR 7.2 billion, driven by a 5.3% and
5.2% increase in number of orders and average order values (AOV) respectively.

events.quote:
1% 8.5% (5.3 ) pp
1: Attributable to shareholders of parent company | * Numbers presented may not add up precisely to the totals provided due to rounding
In FY2025, GMV increased 10.8% YoY to  7.2

events.text: Order count rose to
111.6 million from 106.0 million (+5.3%), while AOV climbed to SAR 64.9 from 61.7 (+5.2%).

events.quote:

events.text: So GMV growth was split evenly between frequency (orders) and ticket size (AOV) — a bal

anced, healthy composition rather than growth bought purely through volume.

### 2. Take rate — the posit

ive surprise
Take rate (Commission Revenue / GMV per the glossary) actually improved.
Commission revenue grew 16.3% to SAR 1,113.8 million, offsetting a 13.1% decline in delivery fee revenue, primarily as a result of the
competitive intensity in the Saudi market.

events.quote:
Commission revenue grew 16.3% to  1,113.8 
million, offsetting a 13.1% decline in delivery fee revenue, primarily as a result of the competitive intensity in 
the Saudi market.

events.text: Working the math: commission take rate rose to
~15.4% in FY2025 from ~14.6% in FY2024 (commission revenue growing 16.3% against
10.8% GMV growth). This reflects a deliberate mix shift —
Revenue declined
8.6% YoY as Jahez responded to evolving market conditions by aligning delivery fees more competitively and increasing its focus on commission-based monetization.

events.quote:
Revenue declined 8.6% YoY as Jahez 
responded to evolving market conditions by aligning delivery fees more competitively and increasing its focus 
on commission - based monetization.

events.text: The trade-off: net revenue growth lagged GMV badly.
Group
net revenue grew 4.7% YoY to SAR 2,323.6 million, driven primarily by strong growth in Non-KSA delivery platforms and the
continued diversification of revenue streams.

events.quote:
Group net revenue grew 4. 7% YoY to  2, 323.6 million, driven primarily by strong growth in Non - KSA delivery 
platforms and the continued diversification of revenue streams. Commission revenue gre

events.text: The 6.1pp gap between GMV growth (10.8%) and net reven

ue growth (4.7%) is the direct fingerprint of delivery-fee compression in KSA.

### 3. AOV & contribution margin (g

ross profit proxy)
Gross profit is the cleanest contribution-margin proxy disclosed.
Gross profit remained resilient at SAR 530.1 million in FY2025 representing a gross margin of 22.8%, down only 1.6
percentage points despite heightened pricing competition. This underscores the benefits of the Group's diversified revenue model and ongoing improvements in delivery cost efficiency
, which helped mitigate the impact of lower delivery fees across the broader markets.

events.quote:
Gross profit remained resilient at  530.1 million in FY2025 representing a gross margin of 22. 8 % , down only 
1.6 percentage point s despite heightened pricing competition. This underscores the be

events.text: A key unit-cost tailwind here
is in-housing delivery via Logi:
Logi's growing in-house delivery cap
acity has been a meaningful contributor to reducing overall per-delivery unit economics for the Group, helping to partially offset the impact of lower delivery fees in the
competitive KSA market. During the year, Logi expanded its fleet to over +4,000 drivers under sponsorship compared to +1,800 drivers
in the prior year. As a result, Logi now accounts for 40% of Jahez deliveries by Q4 2025 with improving un
it cost of delivery.

events.quote:
Importantly, Logi’s growing in - house delivery 
capacity has been a meaningful contributor to reducing overall per - delivery unit economics for the Group, 
helping to partially offset the impact o

in at 193.0 versus 250.0 (-22.8%), with the Adj. EBITDA margin compressing to

The KSA delivery platform segment remained profitable in FY2025, generating net income of SAR 214.8 million, an Adjus
ted EBITDA margin of 11.9%, and a net profit margin of 12.2%.

events.text:
---

### Why net income compressed ~61%
despite +10.8% GMV
Net Profit attributable to shareholders fell to 73.0 from 188.0, a 61.2% decline
, with net margin dropping to 3.1% from 8.5% (-5.3pp).

events.quote:
EBITDA % of Net Revenue 8.3% 11.3% (3.0 ) pp
Net Profit 1 73.0 188.0 (61.2 %)
Net Profit % of Net Revenue 3.

events.text: The compression sits almost entirely *below* gross profit — margin held

there — and stacks up as follows:

**a) OpEx surge (+26
Operating expenses increased to SAR 469.1 million (+26.1% YoY), reflecting higher marketing investment to defend the
Group's share in the existing markets and the consolidation of Snoonu's cost base from Q4 2025.

events.quote:
This underscores the benefits of the Group’s
diversified revenue model and ongoing improvements in delivery cost efficiency, which helped mitigate the 
impact of lower delivery fees across the broad

events.text: With OpEx up 26.1% against

net revenue up only 4.7%, negative operating leverage did the bulk of the damage.

**b) Deliberate prom
otional spend.**
Group Adjusted EBITDA reflecting a deliberate and measured decision to invest
in customer retention and market share defence during a period of elevated industry-wide promotional activity.

events.quote:
o Group Adjusted EBITDA ex ceeded  193 million , reflecting a deliberate and measured decision to 
invest in customer retention and market share defence during a period of elevated industry - wide

events.text: Management framed this
as a conscious trade-off:
net income attributable to shareholders was SAR 73.0 million. The profitability
decline YoY was the result of a deliberate and measured trade-off to invest in retaining customers during a period of elevated promotional activity across the industry.

events.quote:

was  73.0 million. The profitability decline YoY was the result of a deliberate and measured trade - off to 
invest in

events.text: **c) D&A step-up (+75.4%).** From the EBITDA reconciliation,

events.text: This is largely the Logi fleet expansion — the same
in-housing that improves unit delivery cost carries a heavier depreciation load, and Logi it
self swung to a loss:
The segment recorded a net loss of -25.5 million (vs. -7
.8 million in FY 2024), driven primarily by higher depreciation charges as the fleet expands.

events.quote:
The segment recorded a net loss of  - 25.5 million (vs.  - 7.8 million in FY 2024), driven primarily by higher 
depreciation charges as the fleet expands.

events.text: **d) Credit losses & impairments (Other Activ
ities).**
Net loss attributable to shareholders in Other Activities increased to S
AR 82.5 million from 33.0 million in FY 2024. The YoY decline was primarily driven by a significant increase in expected credit losses (ECL), which rose to S
AR 29.4 million in FY 2025 from 0.5 million in the prior year. Additionally, the Group recognized a goodwill impairment on Marn of
SAR 11.8m and higher loss in its Red Color portfolio due to fair value declines.

events.quote:
Adjusted EBITDA losses widened to  25.7 million, while net loss attributable to shareholders 
of parent company increased to  82.5 million from  33.0 million in FY 2024. The YoY decline was primar

events.text: **Note on the offset
that partially cushioned the fall:** below-the-line items moved favorably —
Other
Income/Expense fell to 9.4 from 39.9 (-76.3%) and Financing Cost & Zakat dropped to 12

events.text: Without these, the decline would have been even
steeper; the attributable-shareholder line at -61.2% versus the -68.5% pre-N

CI net profit line reflects minority interests absorbing part of the loss-making segments.

---

**Anal
yst read:** This is a margin story, not a demand story. The demand engine (orders,
AOV, take rate) is intact and monetization is structurally improving toward higher-margin commissions.
The 61% earnings drop is the sum of (i) discretionary mar
keting to defend KSA share, (ii) Snoonu consolidation dragging in a Q4 c
ost base ahead of full revenue capture, (iii) a depreciation-heavy Logi build
-out, and (iv) one-off/non-cash ECL and impairment charges in the investment-ph
ase Other Activities segment. The KSA core delivery platform still ran a 12.2% net margin — the "quality" of the comp
ression is largely investment- and non-recurring-driven rather than core-econom
ics deterioration.
